questions.question: What determines the length of a shadow?
questions.answer: The position of the light source and the distance of the object from it determine the shadow length.

questions.question: Why does a concave mirror form a magnified image for nearby objects?
questions.answer: When the object is within the focal length, reflected rays diverge and appear larger to the eye.

questions.question: Why is lateral inversion observed in plane mirrors?
questions.answer: Because plane mirrors reverse the left and right sides of the image.

questions.question: What is the difference between umbra and penumbra?
questions.answer: Umbra is the fully dark part of a shadow; penumbra is the partially shaded part.

questions.question: Why do convex mirrors give a wider field of view?
questions.answer: Because they diverge light rays, making the reflected area appear smaller and more spread out.

questions.question: How does diffused reflection help in seeing objects clearly?
questions.answer: Diffused reflection scatters light in many directions, allowing the eyes to receive light from all points of an object.

questions.question: Explain why shadows change direction during the day.
questions.answer: Because the Sun appears to move across the sky, changing the angle of light falling on objects.

questions.question: Why is the image in a concave mirror inverted for distant objects?
questions.answer: Because the object is beyond the focal length, reflected rays converge to form a real, inverted image.

questions.question: What is the significance of the focal point of a concave mirror?
questions.answer: It is the point where parallel rays converge, important for magnifying or projecting images.

questions.question: Why are convex mirrors preferred for vehicle rear-view mirrors?
questions.answer: They provide a diminished, upright image and a wider field of view for safety.

questions.question: How does a sundial indicate time?
questions.answer: By the movement of the gnomon's shadow along hour markings as the Sun moves.

questions.question: Explain why plane mirrors do not change image size.
questions.answer: Because the distance of the image from the mirror equals the object distance and angles remain the same.

questions.question: Why do shadows become longer in the morning and evening?
questions.answer: Because the Sun is low in the sky, making light rays strike objects at a shallow angle.

questions.question: How do periscopes allow viewing over obstacles?
questions.answer: By using plane mirrors to reflect light down the tube to the viewer’s eye.

questions.question: What is the difference between regular and diffused reflection?
questions.answer: Regular reflection occurs on smooth surfaces forming clear images; diffused reflection occurs on rough surfaces scattering light.

questions.question: Why does a concave mirror form a real image that can be projected?
questions.answer: Because reflected rays converge at a point in front of the mirror.

questions.question: Why do extended light sources produce blurred shadows?
questions.answer: Because light rays from different points overlap and create a fuzzy edge.

questions.question: What is the effect of a point light source on shadow sharpness?
questions.answer: It produces sharp and well-defined shadows.

questions.question: Why are images in a convex mirror always virtual?
questions.answer: Because reflected rays diverge and do not actually meet, forming a virtual image.

questions.question: Why is diffused reflection necessary for reading text?
questions.answer: It scatters light uniformly from the paper, allowing the eyes to perceive letters clearly.

questions.question: What is the relationship between shadow size and object distance from light?
questions.answer: The closer the object to the light, the larger the shadow; the farther, the smaller.

questions.question: Why is the image in a concave mirror magnified when the object is close?
questions.answer: Because the object is within the focal length, causing diverging rays to appear larger.

questions.question: Why does a convex mirror reduce image size?
questions.answer: Because it diverges rays, causing the reflected image to appear smaller.

questions.question: Why do shadows have a lighter edge called penumbra?
questions.answer: Because some light from the source reaches around the object, partially illuminating the edge.

questions.question: What happens to the shadow when the object is moved closer to the light source?
questions.answer: The shadow becomes larger as light rays diverge more around the object.
